title: The war in Iran has driven the transatlantic relationship further apart
subhed: French President Emmanuel Macron hosted a conference on freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz without the US, as even Washington's closest European partners, including Germany and Italy, are openly beginning to distance themselves.

subhed: On both left and right, talk of primaries continues to stall amid logistical challenges, personal rivalries and deeper strategic voids that experts say traditional parties have yet to confront.

title: Louis Verret paints the drama of football in watercolor
subhed: The artist based in Aubervilliers, a suburb of Paris, paints football's masters as if they are freeze-frames, channeling pure emotion straight to the goal.

title: Why it's important to teach children to recognize birdsong
subhed: Knowing which bird is chirping or warbling is actually child's play, as long as you approach it methodically. We asked experts to guide us in this learning process. Along the way, they gave some compelling reasons to distinguish the blackbird's song from the chaffinch's tune.

title: A photographic journey into the daily lives of home care aides
subhed: Between autumn 2024 and summer 2025, photographer Vincent Gouriou followed several home health aides in Dordogne, southwest France, documenting their often difficult daily routines as they traveled to isolated homes, provided care and offered a listening ear.

title: 'Dansons,' Céline Dion's new single, already feels outdated
subhed: The superstar's new song, written by Jean-Jacques Goldman and launched with great fanfare on Friday, seems to belong to another era. It is far from the hits that the French singer-songwriter created for the Québécois diva in the 1990s.

title: Suspense and pretense: The RN prepares for life after Le Pen
subhed: Pending the July 7 court decision that will determine whether Marine Le Pen can stand in elections, the far-right party must prepare for the 2027 presidential campaign without knowing whether she or Jordan Bardella will be its candidate.
